markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy enters the Exort Series #25 Main Stage BO3 matchup against Wave Esports as the stronger contender, bolstered by Team Spirit's academy infrastructure and a refreshed roster unveiled in February 2026 featuring VILBy, mazay, Kiryasoo, kidofpain, and s1nside under coach S0tF1k, following solid qualification from the Contenders Stage group stage. Wave Esports, a lower-tier DACH squad ranked around #149 on HLTV, showed mixed form with a third-place finish at Esplay Backforce Masters in early March but faltered at Urban Riga Open Season 3 shortly after, lacking the depth to challenge academy-level execution on the active duty map pool. Prior head-to-head favors Spirit Academy with a 2-1 map edge over the last year, though BO3 format in Swiss Round 1 offers Wave upset potential via pistol rounds and eco strategies amid CS2's evolving meta.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 240-259 tweets in May 2026?

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Gensyn FDV above $1.5B one day after launch?

eventMetadata.context_description: Mirassol's commanding 78.5% implied probability stems from their home advantage at Estádio José Maria de Campos Maia in Copa Libertadores Group G, where they sit second with 3 points after a 1-0 win over Lanús and a narrow 0-2 loss away to LDU Quito, while Always Ready languish at the bottom with 0 points from defeats to LDU (0-1) and Lanús (0-1). Bolivian visitors Always Ready face acclimatization challenges descending from high-altitude El Alto to sea-level Brazil, compounded by defender Luis Caicedo's suspension mirroring Mirassol's absent Lucas Oliveira; hosts also miss season-long absentee Negueba and thigh-injured Igor Carius. Despite Mirassol's winless streak in 11 domestic games, traders back their superior group standing and home form against Always Ready's poor away record in the tournament.

markets.question: Will Reform UK win the most seats in the 2026 Scottish Parliament election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Scotland on May 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Scottish Parliament  in this election.

If voting in the Scottish parliamentary election does not occur by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Welsh Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Welsh government, specifically the the Electoral Commission of Scotland (https://www.electoralcommission.org.uk/).

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Holyrood election on May 7, 2026, eight days away, trader consensus overwhelmingly favors the Scottish National Party at 99.1% implied probability for most seats, anchored by late April polls like Survation (17-23 April) showing SNP at 38% constituency vote share versus Reform UK at 20% and Labour at 18%, alongside MRP models projecting SNP 56-67 MSPs under the additional member system—exceeding the 65-seat majority threshold in several scenarios. Steady SNP leads on competence metrics in Ipsos polling (10-20 April) and vulnerabilities limited to marginals like Dumbarton have solidified this positioning amid Reform UK's regional list gains. Late scandals, depressed SNP turnout, or an unforeseen swing remain slim upset risks before polls close.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $6,200 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures (GC) have pulled back sharply to around $4,590 per ounce as of April 29, 2026, down over 2% in the past session amid trader anticipation for the ongoing Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on April 28-29, where signals on the Fed funds rate path could reinforce or temper rate cut expectations supporting non-yielding assets like gold. A weaker U.S. Dollar Index near 98.7 and stable core CPI inflation have provided tailwinds, offset by selective central bank selling for liquidity amid geopolitical strains, though Poland's 20-tonne February purchase underscores ongoing emerging-market demand. Key catalysts ahead include May CPI data and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, with consensus forecasts eyeing $5,000/oz by year-end on persistent safe-haven flows.

markets.question: Spread: Club Atlético de Madrid (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 29 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Club Atlético de Madrid" if Club Atlético de Madrid win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Arsenal FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. Arsenal FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 29 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal advanced to the UEFA Champions League semi-finals with a gritty 1-0 aggregate win over Sporting CP, sealed by a 0-0 second-leg draw at the Emirates amid an injury crisis sidelining Declan Rice, Bukayo Saka, Martin Ødegaard, and others, testing their squad depth just two weeks before facing Atlético Madrid. Atlético, meanwhile, edged Barcelona 3-2 aggregate despite a 1-2 second-leg loss, marking their first semi-final appearance since 2017 under Diego Simeone's defensive mastery at home. Arsenal's 4-0 league-phase thrashing of Atlético earlier this season favors the Gunners' trader consensus, but first-leg Metropolitan Stadium advantage, Simeone's knockout pedigree, and mutual injury concerns—Atlético missing Jan Oblak and Pablo Barrios—promise a tactical battle with rest advantages post-domestic fixtures.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the best Coding AI model at the end of April 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 release on April 16 has driven its 98.7% implied probability as the top coding AI model by month-end, dominating key benchmarks like SWE-bench Verified (82.0% score) and Code Arena, where its variants sweep the top four spots ahead of OpenAI's GPT-5.4 and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro. This agentic coding supremacy stems from superior real-world software engineering task resolution, validated by third-party evaluations and developer feedback on platforms like LMSYS Arena. Trader consensus, backed by significant capital, reflects minimal uncertainty with the market nearing resolution. Realistic challenges include a last-minute OpenAI or Google model drop outperforming on multiple metrics, though historical release timelines make this unlikely before April 30.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, reflecting his executive experience as California governor, national profile from past anti-Trump efforts, and perceived electability in a post-2024 regrouping, despite critiques of state homelessness and high-speed rail issues highlighted in recent media. Former Vice President Kamala Harris, at 7.5%, gained slightly from her April 10 statement considering another run at a National Action Network event, but 2024 campaign baggage tempers support; Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's 8.6% stems from progressive base appeal amid a fragmented field lacking incumbents. Key differentiators include governors' (Newsom, Shapiro, Whitmer) leadership records versus senators' (Ossoff, Kelly) swing-state paths and Harris/AOC's national recognition. Consolidation could follow 2026 midterms, with strong battleground performances, fundraising edges, and DNC endorsements tipping balances in primaries.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of India (Marxist) (CPI(M)) win the most seats in the 2026 Puducherry Legislative Assembly election?
markets.description:
Legislative Assembly elections are to be scheduled to be held in Puducherry, India, in April–June 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly in the 2026 election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. If that also results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (https://eci.gov.in). If multiple official reports differ, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the greatest number of Assembly Constituencies (ACs).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors AINRC at 60.5% to emerge as the leading party in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly election, driven by incumbent Chief Minister N. Rangasamy's popularity and the NDA alliance with BJP, solidified by a late seat-sharing deal contesting most of the 30 seats. Pre-poll surveys from March, like People's Pulse projecting NDA 14-17 seats—near the 16-seat majority threshold—bolster this positioning amid a three-way contest with the INC-DMK alliance and TVK independents. Record 89% voter turnout on April 9 signals high engagement but has not shifted odds toward opposition anti-incumbency narratives; exit polls release today precedes May 4 counting, where coalition math will determine government formation.

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond April 11, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

eventMetadata.context_description: State Rep. Manny Rutinel commands 88% trader consensus as the CO-08 Democratic primary frontrunner ahead of the June 30 vote, bolstered by recent Latino Victory Project independent expenditures exceeding $170,000 in direct support and fresh endorsements from SEIU Colorado and SEIU 105 unions representing over 27,000 state employees, signaling strong backing from key Latino and labor voting blocs in the diverse swing district. Former state Rep. Shannon Bird holds 11% amid ongoing scrutiny of her legislative "no" votes on major Democratic immigration bills, while ex-incumbent Yadira Caraveo and others trail below 3% lacking comparable momentum or fundraising edges reported in recent FEC data. With two months remaining, assembly delegate results and candidate forums could influence turnout in this toss-up race to challenge GOP Rep. Gabe Evans.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In New Jersey's 12th Congressional District Democratic primary on June 2, trader consensus gives plastic surgeon and Army veteran Adam Hamawy a slight edge at 42.5% implied probability, driven by his top fundraising totals reported mid-April and endorsements from Justice Democrats and progressive groups, positioning him as the progressive frontrunner in retiring Rep. Bonnie Watson Coleman's open seat. Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy holds close at 35% on legislative name recognition, while Plainfield Mayor Adrian Mapp sits at 20% bolstered by Union County Democratic backing. The fragmented 13-candidate field and lack of public polling keep the top contenders tightly matched; the April 26 debate among nine participants, including pointed exchanges over Israel policy like Assemblywoman Susan Altman's criticism of Hamawy's Iron Dome stance, amplified scrutiny. County party endorsements, additional fundraising disclosures, and early voting turnout could tip the balance in this battleground primary.

markets.question: Will XRP reach $2.40 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for XRP/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final High price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT High prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance XRP/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 10-14 posts from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between April 24, 12:00 PM ET and May 1, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors fewer than 5 posts from Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's @khamenei_ir X account during April 24–May 1, pricing it at 77.5%, due to sustained inactivity following his martyrdom in US-Israeli airstrikes in late February 2026. The account, now featuring only archival statements from the late Supreme Leader, has posted zero times in this period so far through April 29 and none since April 13, aligning with a sporadic pace of 1–2 posts weekly or less in recent months amid Iran's leadership transition. Higher bins like 10–14 posts at 27.5% reflect minor uncertainty from potential commemorative uploads, but no diplomatic escalations, official announcements, or succession-related events signal an uptick before May 1 resolution.

markets.question: Will Jasmine Paolini win the 2026 Women’s French Open?

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's overwhelming 66.5% implied probability as the 2026 French Open favorite stems from his dominant Monte-Carlo Masters 1000 clay-court title win over Carlos Alcaraz on April 12, marking his first such triumph on the surface as world No. 1, coupled with Alcaraz's April 24 withdrawal from Roland Garros due to a right wrist injury that also sidelined him from Madrid and Rome. This clears a major path for Sinner, who's shown improved clay baseline grinding and topspin adaptation. Alexander Zverev at 7.8% benefits from his proven clay prowess, including multiple Rome titles and deep Roland Garros runs, positioning him as the prime challenger amid a depleted top tier. Novak Djokovic's 5.6% reflects his 3 RG trophies but tempered expectations from modest early clay results and age-related recovery concerns.

markets.question: Will Xiaomi have the second-best Coding AI model at the end of April 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Demeke Mekonnen be the next Prime Minister of Ethiopia?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Ethiopia on June 1, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Ethiopia following the 2026 General elections.

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Ethiopia. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2028, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Ethiopia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed commands 86.5% trader consensus as Ethiopia's next Prime Minister, driven by his Prosperity Party's overwhelming parliamentary dominance since the 2021 general election landslide and firm preparations for the June 1, 2026 parliamentary vote that will select the PM via House of Peoples' Representatives majority. Recent National Election Board of Ethiopia announcements of 10,934 candidates, voter registration openings, and security assessments signal elections proceeding despite Amhara and Oromia conflicts, with Abiy's February parliamentary briefing on mid-year performance and peace-building reinforcing continuity expectations. Lower odds on allies like Oromia President Shimelis Abdisa (3.5%) and Justice Minister Gedion Timothewos reflect their secondary roles absent major shifts, highlighting incumbency advantage in Ethiopia's parliamentary system.

markets.question: Valorant: Team Heretics vs FUT Esports - Map 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Team Heretics and FUT Esports in the VCT EMEA Group Alpha, initially scheduled for April 29 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Heretics" if Team Heretics win Map 1 against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win Map 1 against Team Heretics.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 1. 

If Map 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Valorant: Team Heretics vs FUT Esports (BO3) - VCT EMEA Group Alpha
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Team Heretics and FUT Esports in the VCT EMEA Group Alpha, initially scheduled for April 29 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Heretics" if Team Heretics win the match against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win the match against Team Heretics.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: FUT Esports leads VCT EMEA Stage 1 Group Alpha at 4-0 with an 8-3 map record, reflecting consistent dominance including a 2-1 grind over Karmine Corp last week and prior shutouts against Team Liquid. Team Heretics sits at 2-2 but rides momentum from a roster shakeup on April 8—benching ComeBack for Lithuanian rifler Koshmaras—fueling back-to-back wins: 2-0 versus Gentle Mates on April 24 and 2-1 against Karmine Corp. This Week 5 BO3 carries high playoff stakes, as top-four teams advance; Heretics' revitalized synergy challenges FUT's unbeaten streak in a tightly contested group stage matchup under patch 12.05.

Bayern's dominance is underscored by recent 4-0 head-to-head routs and superior home form, though trader consensus reflects caution amid a mounting injury list—Serge Gnabry sidelined long-term by an adductor tear, joined by Lennart Karl (hamstring) and others—thinning attacking depth during a congested schedule featuring Champions League semis against PSG. events.description:

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: CFTC's March 2026 staff advisory provides the clearest guidance yet for designated contract markets (DCMs) to self-certify sports event contracts, urging pre-filing engagement with staff and leagues like MLB to address manipulation and integrity risks amid surging prediction market volumes. An early April Third Circuit ruling affirmed CFTC's exclusive jurisdiction, preempting state gambling laws and blocking enforcement against DCM-traded sports outcomes, significantly lowering barriers. While Kalshi self-certified golf and tennis contracts that month, no further major filings have emerged in the past 30 days, leaving trader focus on whether incumbents like CME or challengers including Aristotle, Railbird, and LedgerX act before the June 30 deadline, per public CFTC records. Ongoing Ninth Circuit arguments in Nevada add uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

markets.question: Cagliari: Emilio Nava vs Damir Dzumhur
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Emilio Nava and Damir Dzumhur in the Cagliari, originally scheduled for April 28, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Emilio Nava' if Emilio Nava advances against Damir Dzumhur.

This market will resolve to 'Damir Dzumhur' if Damir Dzumhur advances against Emilio Nava.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Cagliari Challenger round of 32 on red clay, higher-ranked Damir Dzumhur (No. 84) meets Emilio Nava (No. 116) in their second career encounter, following Nava's straight-sets win over Dzumhur at last October's Bratislava 2 Challenger on indoor hard—their first clay matchup. Both arrive with momentum from Madrid Masters last week: Nava reached the third round, defeating compatriot Jenson Brooksby and Valentin Vacherot before a loss to Arthur Fils, while Dzumhur advanced to the second round via a win over Mattia Bellucci prior to falling to Tallon Griekspoor. Dzumhur's veteran consistency and stronger historical clay record contrast Nava's rising power game and recent form; no injuries reported, with outdoor Sardinia conditions emphasizing endurance in best-of-three sets.

markets.question: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

events.title: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty at 96.7% for no divorce announcement by June 30, 2026, anchored by the Clintons' enduring 50-year marriage and absence of any public statements, legal filings, or confirmed separations despite unsubstantiated tabloid rumors in late 2025 tied to Jeffrey Epstein documents. Buoying confidence, Bill and Hillary Clinton made a rare joint public appearance on April 20, 2026, at the Boston Marathon finish line to celebrate daughter Chelsea's completion, with Bill embracing her and placing her medal amid family photos signaling unity. No developments in the past 30 days suggest discord; their history of navigating scandals reinforces stability. Late-breaking scenarios like health crises or unforeseen legal revelations could shift odds, though structural barriers remain high given their ages and public roles.

markets.question: Will A Just Russia – For Truth (SRZP) win the second-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) for second-most seats in the September 2026 State Duma election at 34.5%, closely trailed by New People (NL) at 31% and Liberal Democratic Party of Russia (LDPR) at 26.5%, reflecting a tight contest among systemic opposition parties behind dominant United Russia. Recent VCIOM polls through mid-April show NL surging to 13.4% in proportional list voting intentions, overtaking LDPR (10.1%) and matching KPRF (10.9%), fueled by party conventions and Kremlin-backed promotion as a middle-class alternative amid economic pressures eroding ER support. KPRF holds an edge via historical strength in single-member districts, but regional arrests of its activists and LDPR's nationalist appeals keep the race fragmented. Separation could arise from federal party list approvals, electronic primaries, or shifts in undecided voters (10-20%) ahead of the campaign launch.

markets.question: Will the price of XRP be between $1.60 and $1.70 on May 2?
markets.description:

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin Group D battle, with co-host USA and Türkiye deadlocked at 35% implied probabilities to top the standings, driven by USA's home-soil edges in all three matches—starting June 12 versus Paraguay at SoFi Stadium—and Türkiye's surging form from their late March playoff win over Kosovo, featuring stars like Hakan Çalhanoğlu, Kenan Yıldız, and Arda Güler. Recent friendlies underscore the parity: USA split results with Japan (0-1 loss away, 3-0 home win mid-April) after beating Paraguay and Australia 2-1 last year but falling 2-1 to Türkiye; Australia's FIFA Series triumphs (1-0 over Cameroon, 5-1 Curaçao end March) boost their physicality to 7.5%, while Paraguay's gritty CONMEBOL qualifiers and 1-2 friendly loss to Morocco position them third at 17% amid low-block threats and set-piece prowess. Head-to-head dynamics and no major injuries keep the race wide open.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 140-159 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Diego Dedura-Palomero vs. Matthew William Donald: Total Sets O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Diego Dedura-Palomero and Matthew William Donald in the Mauthausen, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 11:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger statistics.

events.title: Mauthausen: Diego Dedura-Palomero vs Matthew William Donald
events.description:

This market will resolve to 'Diego Dedura-Palomero' if Diego Dedura-Palomero advances against Matthew William Donald.

This market will resolve to 'Matthew William Donald' if Matthew William Donald advances against Diego Dedura-Palomero.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

President Nataša Pirc Musar subsequently directed lawmakers on April 25 to nominate a prime minister-designate capable of building such a coalition within 14 days, or risk a relative majority vote and potential snap election. Traders price Janša at 88% implied probability due to SDS's strong position to lead right-wing coalition talks with parties like NSi (Anže Logar) or others, bolstered by his prior premiership experience amid Golob's failed negotiations; alternatives trail as less viable absent major shifts.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Ballroom" during King Charles visit?
markets.description:
King Charles is scheduled to visit the White House from April 27 to April 30, 2026 (https://time.com/article/2026/04/01/king-charles-camilla-royal-state-visit-washington-america-trump/). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during his appearance at events featuring King Charles from April 27 through April 30, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

This market is explicitly about events featuring both Donald Trump and King Charles from April 27 through April 30, 2026. All such events featuring both on these dates count. Speeches, events, or comments which feature one named individual but not the other, will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the events contain a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this visit is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the top AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Bank of Japan increases interest rates by 25 bps after the June 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for June is scheduled to be released on June 16, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's June 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

The BOJ upgraded its fiscal 2026 core inflation forecast to 2.8% from 1.9%—sustained above the 2% target—while trimming GDP growth to 0.5%, bolstering normalization expectations despite yen weakness and geopolitical risks that buoy the 40% no-change odds. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Sunderland at 41.5% implied probability for the Premier League clash at Molineux, driven by Wolverhampton Wanderers' dismal campaign as 20th-placed basement dwellers with just 17 points from 34 matches, already relegated amid a mounting injury crisis including goalkeeper José Sá's shoulder spasm doubt, Ladislav Krejčí's neck issue, Sam Johnstone's season-ending absence, and Yerson Mosquera's suspension. Sunderland, comfortably mid-table around 12th with 46 points, builds momentum from a strong win over Wolves earlier this season and recent form outpacing the hosts, despite their own absences like Dan Ballard and Romaine Mundle. Wolves' home advantage and head-to-head parity offer upset potential, keeping Wolves at 30.5% and draw at 28% viable in this competitive matchup.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) hit (LOW) $260 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Apple (AAPL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Apple (AAPL) hit Week of April 27 2026?
events.description: What will Apple (AAPL) hit Week of April 27 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Apple's share price has hovered between $265 and $273 during the week of April 27, closing at $267.61 on April 27 before rebounding to around $270.71 amid heightened volatility tied to tomorrow's fiscal Q2 2026 earnings release on April 30, with a conference call at 5 p.m. ET. Trader sentiment reflects optimism around resilient iPhone sales defying a global smartphone slump, AI integration progress, and record recycled materials in products, tempered by recent leadership shifts announcing Tim Cook as executive chairman and John Ternus as CEO effective September 2026. Consensus anticipates steady revenue growth, with focus on services margins and guidance; post-earnings reaction could swing the stock toward key levels like $268 or $272, ahead of WWDC in June.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the South Carolina Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm South Carolina U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Senator Lindsey Graham's strong fundraising—over $11 million cash on hand—and endorsements from Donald Trump and Tim Scott solidify Republican dominance in South Carolina's deep-red Senate race, driving trader consensus to 79% for a GOP victory. Recent polls, including a March Impact Research survey showing Graham leading likely Democratic nominee Annie Andrews 47%-42%, reflect a competitive but structurally tilted contest amid the state's GOP supermajority and historical incumbent advantages. Paul Dans' April 10 withdrawal and endorsement of primary challenger Mark Lynch, where Graham leads 41%-21%, has prompted Inside Elections to shift the rating to Likely Republican on April 23. June 9 primaries loom as the next catalyst, with the general election November 3.

markets.question: Will Girona qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Champions League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Champions League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 LaLiga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Champions League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or LaLiga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Massachusetts Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's dominant performance at the Massachusetts Republican Party's state convention in Worcester over the April 26-27 weekend propelled his trader consensus to 87%, as he secured the party's endorsement with 70% of delegate votes and easily cleared the 15% ballot qualification threshold. Brian Shortsleeve also qualified at around 15-20%, holding steady at 4.9%, while Mike Kennealy fell short, prompting his campaign suspension and dropping his odds to 2%. Minogue's massive fundraising edge—nearly $10 million cash on hand versus competitors' under $1.5 million—bolsters his frontrunner status ahead of the September 1 Republican primary, where the endorsement historically aids nominees in low-turnout contests, though Shortsleeve's MBTA executive background keeps a slim challenge alive.

markets.question: Will Chelsea Clinton be the democratic nominee for NY-12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: NY-12 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely contested NY-12 Democratic primary on June 23 to succeed retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler, trader consensus gives Assemblymember Micah Lasher a 45% implied probability lead, fueled by key endorsements from Nadler and Gov. Kathy Hochul (April 13) plus $5 million from Michael Bloomberg's super PAC, bolstering his fundraising edge in this deep-blue Manhattan district. Assemblymember Alex Bores trails at 32.5%, competitive per his internal Hart Research poll (March, Schlossberg 22%, Bores 19%, Lasher 14%, 28% undecided), while Jack Schlossberg's 22% reflects Kennedy family name recognition amid forums spotlighting his relative inexperience. Recent April candidate debates at 92nd Street Y and elsewhere sharpened East Side-West Side divides among top contenders, with no new public polls shifting the fluid race.

markets.question: Will Yoo Dong-soo win the 2026 Incheon mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Incheon mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Incheon, South Korea on June 3, 2026 to elect the next mayor of Incheon. 

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party of Korea candidate Park Chan-dae commands trader consensus at 94.5% implied probability for the June 3 Incheon mayoral election, driven by consistent double-digit polling leads over incumbent People Power Party Mayor Yoo Jeong-bok seeking a third term, including recent surveys showing Park at 48-49% to Yoo's 33-35%. National momentum favors the Democratic Party across metropolitan races, with Incheon party support nearing 50%, bolstered by Park's profile as a three-term lawmaker and former floor leader aligned with President Lee Jae-myung. Yoo's April 29 candidacy launch drew crowds but faces scrutiny over past controversies. Upsets could arise from scandals, low Democratic turnout, or Yoo's late surge on incumbency, though trends suggest formidable barriers.

markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the player who is awarded the 2025–26 NHL Vezina Trophy.

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Vezina Trophy, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: NHL Vezina Trophy Winner
events.description:

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Vezina Trophy, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Andrei Vasilevskiy's league-leading 39 wins, second-best 2.31 GAA, and top-three .912 save percentage across 58 starts have solidified trader consensus at nearly 90% implied probability for the Vezina Trophy, reflecting his dominant regular-season workload and NHL.com panel endorsements that crowned him the overwhelming choice post-season finale. Tampa Bay's strong finish amplified his case over challengers like Scott Wedgewood (elite GAA leader but fewer wins), Jeremy Swayman, and Ilya Sorokin, whose advanced metrics trailed in recent polls. With finalists announced today (April 29), a realistic challenge could arise from GM voting quirks favoring Wedgewood's goals saved above expected or Sorokin's late surge if traditional stats underweight situational factors.

markets.question: Will O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, O'Reilly Automotive is estimated to release earnings on April 29, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for O'Reilly Automotive's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.70 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if O'Reilly Automotive reports GAAP EPS greater than $0.70 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If O'Reilly Automotive releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be 7°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Map Handicap: GH (-1.5) vs ODDIK Academy (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Game Hunters and ODDIK Academy in the CCT South America Series #1 Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 28 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Game Hunters" if Game Hunters wins 2 or more maps than ODDIK Academy in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "ODDIK Academy". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: GH (-1.5) vs ODDIK Academy (+1.5)

events.title: Counter-Strike: Game Hunters vs ODDIK Academy (BO3) - CCT South America Series #1 Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Game Hunters and ODDIK Academy in the CCT South America Series #1 Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 28 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Game Hunters" if Game Hunters win the match against ODDIK Academy.

This market will resolve to "ODDIK Academy" if ODDIK Academy win the match against Game Hunters.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: GameHunters head into this CCT South America Series #1 group stage BO3 as the higher-ranked side at #134 HLTV, buoyed by mixed recent form including a 13-2 rout of Crashers and 13-3 over Yawara in Odyssey Cup Brazil 2026, though tempered by losses to Imperial (1-2) and Shinden (0-2) in ESL Challenger League Season 51 South America Cup 4 over the past week. ODDIK Academy, unranked with peak #201 and no matches since late 2025, fields academy prospects like Ceruttera and PremiuM but lacks competitive reps, tilting trader consensus toward GameHunters' edge in map vetoes on strongholds like Dust2 (57% win rate) and Ancient (54%). Swiss format stakes advancement on round differential; no roster shifts or patch changes noted.

markets.question: Ayo Dosunmu: Points O/U 19.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 30 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ayo Dosunmu scores more than 19.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Ayo Dosunmu scores 19.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Nuggets vs. Timberwolves
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 30 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Nuggets win, the market will resolve to "Nuggets".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves hold a 3-2 series lead over the Nuggets in their Western Conference first-round matchup, with Game 6 set for Target Center on April 30, but Minnesota faces massive hurdles without star guard Anthony Edwards (left knee bruise, out multiple weeks) and Donte DiVincenzo (Achilles tear, season-ending), losses sustained around Game 4 that severely thin their backcourt. Denver capitalized in Game 5's 125-113 road rout, shooting efficiently while forcing 25 Timberwolves turnovers amid Aaron Gordon's ongoing calf absence and Peyton Watson's hamstring issue, extending their 12-1 home playoff streak momentum into this elimination spot for Minnesota. Jokic's dominance and Nuggets' historical edge (92-61 all-time) contrast Wolves' home-court edge and depth reliance on Ayo Dosunmu, heightening upset potential in a closely contested series.

markets.question: XRP Up or Down - April 30, 8:50AM-8:55AM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the XRP price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the XRP/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/xrp-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream XRP/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, held April 16 to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill, has solidified at approximately 20.7% after late-counted mail and provisional ballots from Democratic strongholds like Passaic County boosted her margin from an initial sub-20% at 95% reporting. This outcome in the safely blue suburban district—where Mejia outperformed Kamala Harris's 2024 presidential result by over 12 points—reflects strong progressive turnout against Republican Joe Hathaway, despite softer support in moderate towns like Livingston and Millburn with large Jewish populations wary of her left-wing Israel stance. Trader consensus prices a 20-25% victory at 91.5%, with final certification expected soon; rare shifts could arise from unresolved ballots or a recount, though no disputes have emerged.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (LOW) $300 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.
